name: Free AI Voice Generator using Edge TTS

parameters.content:
## 🎓 HOW THIS WORKFLOW WORKS
1. Student types text in chat
2. ⚙️ Settings node stores YOUR folder path + voice (edit ONCE here)
3. Code node converts text into a real .txt file
4. That file is saved to your computer
5. Edge TTS (free, offline, no API key) reads the file and creates an MP3
6. The MP3 is read back into n8n so you can preview / send it anywhere

⚠️ PREREQUISITES:
- Must run n8n SELF-HOSTED (Desktop app or Docker). n8n Cloud blocks 'Execute Command' + file system access for security.
- Install Edge TTS on your machine: pip install edge-tts
- Create the folder you set in the Settings node BEFORE running (e.g. mkdir on your PC)

## ⚙️ STEP 1 — EDIT THESE 2 VALUES
folderPath: where files get saved on YOUR computer
voiceName: which Edge TTS voice to use

Try other voices by running in terminal:
edge-tts --list-voices

Popular ones:
- en-US-GuyNeural (male)
- en-US-JennyNeural (female)
- en-GB-RyanNeural (British male)

parameters.command: =edge-tts --file "{{ $('⚙️ Settings (Edit Me First!)').item.json.folderPath }}/narration.txt" --voice "{{ $('⚙️ Settings (Edit Me First!)').item.json.voiceName }}" --write-media "{{ $('⚙️ Settings (Edit Me First!)').item.json.folderPath }}/narration.mp3"

parameters.content:
## ✅ DONE!
This node outputs the finished MP3 as binary data.

From here, students can chain on:
- 'Respond to Chat' node to preview it
- Send to Telegram/Discord
- Upload to Google Drive
- Feed into a video-editing automation

This is the natural 'Part 2' cliffhanger for your next lesson.

# Run in Node.js Command Prompt 

 set N8N_ENFORCE_SETTINGS_FILE_PERMISSIONS=false
 set N8N_BINARY_DATA_MODEL=filesystem
 set N8N_RESTRICT_FILE_ACCESS_TO=C:\n8n-files\voices;
